repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: nvim_set_hl always replaces all attributes.

Solution: Add update field. When true, merge with existing attributes instead of replacing. Unspecified attributes are preserved. If highlight group doesn't exist, falls back to reset mode.

comment_data.body: I think this should handle `ns_id=0` , eg move the logic at the call site here. That would be symmetric with `ns_hl_def()` .
is it documented that `fg=-1` should be used with `update=true` to explicitly unset one specific color?

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -162,6 +162,7 @@ API
   `style='minimal'` or `:setlocal statusline=` to hide the statusline.
 • Added experimental |nvim__exec_lua_fast()| to allow remote API clients to
   execute code while nvim is blocking for input.
+• |nvim_set_hl()| supports update specified attributes.

comment_data.body: it doesn't really "merge", right? it only sets the given keys, and preserves existing keys.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1589,6 +1585,10 @@ nvim_set_hl({ns_id}, {name}, {val})                            *nvim_set_hl()*
                  • underdotted: boolean
                  • underdouble: boolean
                  • underline: boolean
+                 • update: When true, merge with existing attributes instead

comment_data.body: this makes the code very hard to follow, easy to miss this redefinition in the middle of the function. why not just rename the first `CHECK_FLAG` ?

this sure seems similar, semantically, to `hl_get_ui_attr` https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/b02d1303b80f18f482b9eee94f1f29245c9da69f/src/nvim/highlight.c#L297

the current docstring "Get highlight attributes for a highlight group" doesn't give a hint about how they're different.

yes, they have slightly different parameters and implementations, but are both actually needed?

if both are needed, next question is why one lives here and the other lives in highlight.c
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -3196,3 +3206,38 @@ int name_to_ctermcolor(const char *name)
   TriState bold = kNone;
   return lookup_color(i, false, &bold);
 }
+
+/// Get highlight attributes for a highlight group
+///
+/// @param ns_id Namespace ID (0 for global namespace)
+/// @param hl_id Highlight group ID (1-based)
+/// @param[out] attrs Pointer to store the attributes
+/// @return true if highlight group exists and has valid attributes
+bool hl_ns_get_attrs(int ns_id, int hl_id, HlAttrs *attrs)

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
  it('update=true sets only specified keys', function()
```

are hex literals supported by puc lua? 

for readability, can use `vim.fn.printf()`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -257,6 +257,40 @@ describe('API: set highlight', function()
     )
     assert_alive()
   end)
+
+  it('can update specific attributes with update flag', function()
+    api.nvim_set_hl(0, 'TestGroup', { fg = '#ff0000', bg = '#0000ff', bold = true })
+    api.nvim_set_hl(0, 'TestGroup', { bg = '#00ff00', update = true })
+    local hl = api.nvim_get_hl(0, { name = 'TestGroup' })
+    eq(0xff0000, hl.fg)

comment_data.body: hl_ns_get_attrs is essentially the core of hl_get_ui_attr without the pumblend/optional UI logic. I've refactored hl_get_ui_attr to call hl_ns_get_attrs internally
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -3196,3 +3206,38 @@ int name_to_ctermcolor(const char *name)
   TriState bold = kNone;
   return lookup_color(i, false, &bold);
 }
+
+/// Get highlight attributes for a highlight group
+///
+/// @param ns_id Namespace ID (0 for global namespace)
+/// @param hl_id Highlight group ID (1-based)
+/// @param[out] attrs Pointer to store the attributes
+/// @return true if highlight group exists and has valid attributes
+bool hl_ns_get_attrs(int ns_id, int hl_id, HlAttrs *attrs)

comment_regular_data.body: I think this should be fine to add to the 0.12 public API (just need to resolve https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37546#discussion_r2821016066, otherwise there isn't much design questions), or what do you think @justinmk ?

title: feat(api): add update to nvim_set_hl
